title: Ephemerantha guttenbergii
taxonomy: Ephemerantha guttenbergii is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is further classified into the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. The genus Ephemerantha is sometimes combined with the genus Cranichis, but it is still recognized as a distinct genus by many sources. Ephemerantha guttenbergii is a terrestrial orchid that grows in shaded areas, often near streams and rivers.

intro: Ephemerantha guttenbergii, also known as the goldenroot or golden seal, is a perennial plant belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha guttenbergii include berberine, gentiopicroside, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is commonly used in tinctures, capsules, and teas, as well as in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
